Vivid Electromech Limited Reports 36% YoY Revenue Growth in Q1 FY27 - Order Pipeline Strong at ₹210 Cr

Vivid Electromech Limited has delivered a strong performance in Q1 FY27, reflecting continued execution momentum across data centres, infrastructure, renewable energy and industrial projects.

Revenue grew to ₹32.68 Cr in Q1 FY27, up ~36% from ₹24.05 Cr in Q1 FY26, while the order pipeline remained robust at ₹210 Cr - providing strong revenue visibility for the coming quarters.

During the quarter, Key business developments included:
* Received a ₹30.13 Cr order from STT Global Data Centres India Private Limited for SITC of PDU and DWDM Panels
* Secured a ₹20.24 Cr order from Univastu India Limited for MV switchgear, panels and distribution boards
* Continued progress on the 1,20,000 sq. ft. automated manufacturing facility at Ambernath

This performance reflects Vivid Electromech Limited's continued commitment to expanding its presence across data centres, infrastructure and renewable energy - backed by strong OEM partnerships with ABB, Siemens, Hitachi and Lauritz Knudsen and a robust pipeline of high-value projects.

Captain Polyplast Limited has secured a fresh order from Maharashtra State Electricity Distribution Company Limited (MSEDCL) for 1,000 solar pumps valued at ₹23.6 Cr (inclusive of GST) under the PM-KUSUM B Scheme.

Order at a Glance:
* Awarded by: Maharashtra State Electricity Distribution Company Limited (MSEDCL)
* Scope: Design, Manufacture, Supply, Installation, Testing and Commissioning of 1,000 Off-Grid DC Solar Photovoltaic Water Pumping Systems under PM-KUSUM B Scheme
* Order Value: ₹23.6 Cr (inclusive of GST)

Solar EPC Momentum:
This order reinforces Captain Polyplast Limited's growing presence in the government-led solar EPC space, supporting India's vision of clean energy-driven agriculture while enhancing revenue visibility and strengthening the Company's solar EPC order book.

This milestone further cements Captain Polyplast Limited's position as a preferred partner for state-level agencies in the solar pumping space - reinforcing its commitment to delivering quality renewable energy solutions across India.

Starting August 3, 2026, the way stock closing prices are calculated will change.

SEBI is introducing a Closing Auction Session (CAS), starting with stocks that trade in the F&O segment.

Instead of using the current VWAP-based method, buy and sell orders will be brought together in an auction to determine the official closing price.

From August 3, these will be the market timings:

- Stocks under CAS: Regular trading will end at 3:15 PM, followed by the Closing Auction Session until 3:35 PM.

- Stocks not under CAS: Regular trading will continue until 3:30 PM.

- Equity and index derivatives: Trading will continue until 3:40 PM.

Here’s all you need to know about CAS👇
https://youtu.be/h2_ic-pCzB4?si=dHRGVlGkAW1-ARB9

Anand Rathi Wealth : Management says that they can grow their AUM by 20-25% in the long term. If, by any chance, they are able to grow their AUM by 26% consistently, their AUM can become 10 times in 10 years. 

Q1 FY27 con call insights : 

1. For the Q1 FY27 quarter, company highlighted zero regret RM attrition rate. This means that no Relationship Managers left the company during this period, and those who left were not considered to be a major loss to the company's operations.

2. Whenever a RM leaves a current firm and joins its competitors' firm, the RM takes with it the clients. To counter this, the company employs a process-driven approach which helps them to retain the AUM managed by the RM into their own firm. This prevents the fall in AUM for the company.

3. The company's platinum clients are those who invest more than Rs 50 cr. The number of platinum clients went up from 50 to 230 within two years. It is expected to reach 450-500 over the next two years.

4. Company aims for a 20-25% annual growth in AUM, which will be primarily composed of two factors : 10-12% Mark-to-Market gains, and 10-12% targeted net inflows.

5. The company currently recommend to their clients only mutual funds. They currently do not recommend individual stocks, gold, or AIFs as of now. They currently have 14 schemes, and all have beaten Nifty by 6% in Q1.

6. A major chunk of the Rs 110 cr reported in other income is due to the mark-to-market gains.

7. The company has started operations in the UK because they see a huge potential in the NRI market.

8. Anand Rathi has no plans to enter into investment banking.

9. The company has received board approval to apply for a Mutual Fund Asset Management Company (AMC) license.

10. Close to 6,500 additional client families can be handled by the existing RMs they have.

#HEG | Demerger Special Situation

Sometimes the market does not value a company properly because too many different businesses sit inside one structure.

Stay till the end and let us know which of the demerged business you would want to be in!!

-------------------

HEG looks like one such case.

Today, HEG is a mix of:

• Graphite electrodes  
• Hydro power  
• Battery anode materials  
• Battery energy storage solutions  
• Solar + BESS renewable platform  

Now the company is splitting this into two focused listed entities.

1. HEG Limited

This will house the mature graphite electrode business.

FY26 revenue: ~₹2,568 Cr  
FY26 EBITDA: ~₹497 Cr  
EBITDA margin: ~19%

Capacity is being expanded from 1,00,000 TPA to 1,15,000 TPA by early 2028.

This remains the cash-generating industrial business.

2. HEG Greentech

This will house the future-facing clean energy platform.

It includes:

• 278 MW hydro assets  
• TACC battery anode materials  
• REPlus battery energy solutions  
• Solar + BESS IPP platform  

The interesting part?

The hydro assets alone are expected to generate ~₹300 Cr+ annual free cash flow.

So Greentech is not starting from zero.

3. The big optionality

TACC is setting up India’s first large-scale battery-grade synthetic graphite anode material facility.

Phase 1: 20,000 TPA  
Commercial start: April 2027  
Long-term target: 60,000 TPA by FY32

If this executes well, the market may stop looking at HEG only as a graphite electrode company.

4. Why this can create opportunity

Right now, the market is valuing one combined entity.

But post-demerger, investors may get two separate stories:

• A mature graphite electrode business  
• A hydro-backed Greentech platform  

That separation itself can unlock better valuation discovery.

5. Near-term trigger

The key event to watch is NCLT approval.

Management’s latest guidance is Q2 FY27.

Post that, the focus shifts to record date, listing timeline and separate valuation of both entities.

Simple takeaway:

HEG demerger is not just a split.

It is a chance for the market to revalue two very different businesses separately.

One is cash-generating.

The other is optionality-heavy.

That is what makes this special situation worth tracking.

Not a recommendation.

Zee Entertainment just removed a major financial overhang.

The company has received RBI approval to:
✅ Redeem its outstanding USD 23.9 million Foreign Currency Convertible Bonds (FCCBs)
✅ Cancel the remaining unused USD 215.1 million FCCB commitment

Why does this matter?
 - Removes a long-standing balance sheet overhang.
 - Eliminates potential future dilution from FCCB conversions.
 - Simplifies the company's capital structure.
 - Gives investors better visibility on future capital allocation.

This may not immediately impact earnings, but cleaning up the balance sheet is often an underrated positive, especially for companies focused on improving governance and shareholder returns.

Sometimes, reducing financial complexity creates as much value as growing revenues.

Aerospace Contract Manufacturing is structurally superior to Pharma CDMO.

Both are Contract Manufacturing businesses at their core, but the quality and duration of earnings are very different.

A Pharma CDMO molecule typically offers 10-15 years of visibility before patent expiry creates revenue reset risk.

An aerospace programme can run for 50-70 years, offers 4-5x longer revenue visibility, carries prohibitive switching costs and generates decades of aftermarket revenues.

Longer visibility. Lower reset risk. Stickier customers. More durable earnings.

That is why aerospace contract manufacturing deserves higher value.

Source: Nuvama Research, Aequs initiation report.

HEG | Demerger Special Situation

Sometimes the market does not value a company properly because too many different businesses sit inside one structure.

HEG looks like one such case.

Today, HEG is a mix of:

• Graphite electrodes  
• Hydro power  
• Battery anode materials  
• Battery energy storage solutions  
• Solar + BESS renewable platform  

Now the company is splitting this into two focused listed entities.

1. HEG Limited

This will house the mature graphite electrode business.

FY26 revenue: ~₹2,568 Cr  
FY26 EBITDA: ~₹497 Cr  
EBITDA margin: ~19%

Capacity is being expanded from 1,00,000 TPA to 1,15,000 TPA by early 2028.

This remains the cash-generating industrial business.

2. HEG Greentech

This will house the future-facing clean energy platform.

It includes:

• 278 MW hydro assets  
• TACC battery anode materials  
• REPlus battery energy solutions  
• Solar + BESS IPP platform  

The interesting part?

The hydro assets alone are expected to generate ~₹300 Cr+ annual free cash flow.

So Greentech is not starting from zero.

3. The big optionality

TACC is setting up India’s first large-scale battery-grade synthetic graphite anode material facility.

Phase 1: 20,000 TPA  
Commercial start: April 2027  
Long-term target: 60,000 TPA by FY32

If this executes well, the market may stop looking at HEG only as a graphite electrode company.

4. Why this can create opportunity

Right now, the market is valuing one combined entity.

But post-demerger, investors may get two separate stories:

• A mature graphite electrode business  
• A hydro-backed Greentech platform  

That separation itself can unlock better valuation discovery.

5. Near-term trigger

The key event to watch is NCLT approval.

Management’s latest guidance is Q2 FY27.

Post that, the focus shifts to record date, listing timeline and separate valuation of both entities.

Simple takeaway:

HEG demerger is not just a split.

It is a chance for the market to revalue two very different businesses separately.

One is cash-generating.

The other is optionality-heavy.

That is what makes this special situation worth tracking.

Not a recommendation.

First, whether the US business recovers now that tariffs have eased back toward 18% and the first refund cheque (USD 540,785) has arrived. 

Second, whether the record order book of ₹912 crore (about 1.24 times FY26 revenue, roughly two thirds of it overseas) converts into shipped, profitable revenue rather than sitting stuck in working capital. 

Third, whether the company can fund its growth without its cash getting swallowed by receivables and inventory, because it collects slowly and holds a lot of stock.

Gold loans are the next big lending opportunity.

The segment is expected to grow at 12–15% CAGR over the next 4–5 years, driven by increasing demand for secured credit and everyone wants to be a part of it.

Now, Tata Capital is making its move.

It is acquiring an 88.6% stake in Yogakshemam Loans (Yogloans) for ₹93 crore, marking its entry into the gold loan business.

This also signals that competition is set to intensify for established players like Muthoot Finance, Manappuram Finance, Capri Global and other gold-focused NBFCs and banks.

When large, well-capitalised institutions enter a niche, it's usually a sign that the opportunity has become too big to ignore.

Gold loans could be one of the fastest-growing lending segments of this decade.

India strategy turned more positive: Target Nifty 26,500 by Jun'27 with expectation of FII flows returning and domestic recovery strengthening #ym

*Key calls are* Value over Growth, Large Caps over Mid Caps, Banks/Utilities/Tourism/Defense as preferred themes, while Metals, Cement, IT/Pharma exporters and rural plays are less favored 

Sector changes: 

*Upgraded
> Utilities → Overweight 
> Industrials → Market Weight 

*Downgraded
> Staples → Market Weight (from a more positive stance) 
> Metals & Mining → Underweight 
> Cement → Underweight 

*Unchanged positive
> Banks → Overweight
> Energy Refiners → Overweight 
> TMT → Overweight 
> Defense → Overweight 

*Unchanged negative
> IT Exporters → Underweight 
> Pharma Exporters → Underweight
> OMCs/Downstream Oil → Underweight

Goldman Sachs -India Strategy: Room to rebound  
India’s outlook has improved in recent weeks, amid lower commodity prices, stabilized currency, resilient domestic growth, healthy earnings expectations in 2Q, and potential recovery in select domestic pockets. With ultra-light foreign positioning, we see ample room for flows to return. While renewed geopolitical tensions in the Middle East may fuel near-term volatility, we see room for NIFTY to recover towards our June 2027 target of 26,500, implying c.10% upside from current levels following a 9% drawdown in 1H

Structurally, we remain bullish on defense and energy security. Sectorally, we raise utilities to overweight and remain overweight on banks, energy refiners, TMT, and defense. We
stay underweight exporters, downstream oil and downgrade select materials to underweight

reports.text:
Senores Pharma - Building the US Playbook 🔥

• US remains the core growth engine, with management targeting ~70% of revenue from the US going forward

• Strong ANDA pipeline with 97 products:
  >> 58 approved (22 launched, 36 to be launched in the next 8-10 quarters)
  >> 39 products under development (5 already filed)

• Expanding addressable market through front-end JVs:
  >> Zoraya (51% owned) – direct marketing
  >> Amerisyn (70% owned) – government/federal supply
  >> Expansion comes with minimal incremental capex

• APNAR facility approved by USFDA, Health Canada, and UK MHRA, supporting future US growth.

• CDMO business scaling up:
  >> Commercialized molecules increased from 13 to 16
  >> Pipeline of 20-25 contracted products

• Significant R&D push:
  >> Headcount to rise from 50 to 120
  >> New 30,000 sq. ft. R&D center
  >> FY27 R&D spend guided at 6-7% of sales

Source - Choice Institutional Equities
